family: Support, options, numerical relations, and local structure

family: Support, options, numerical relations, and local structure
justification: local_number_relation defender majority (mirror of overload).

family: Carrying, dribbling, evasion, and ball progression on foot

family: Carrying, dribbling, evasion, and ball progression on foot

family: Carrying, dribbling, evasion, and ball progression on foot
justification: Observed carry interval from carry_episode plus multi_line_model/relative_position_to_line can express a carrier moving from not beyond to beyond an observed ranked line. This does not claim defender-bypass causation.
composition_constraint_note: Requires same carrier identity and line measured at the declared carry start/end frames.

family: Carrying, dribbling, evasion, and ball progression on foot
justification: carry_episode emits displacement_m for observed movement-under-control between controlled reception and the terminal same-player action.

family: Carrying, dribbling, evasion, and ball progression on foot
justification: carry_episode emits carry_duration_seconds for the observed movement-under-control interval.

family: Carrying, dribbling, evasion, and ball progression on foot
justification: carry_episode now anchors the carry and time_to_arrival can score static target reachability, but generated open-space/space-region selection is not yet a declared capability.
closest_supported_substitute: carry_episode + time_to_arrival to a declared target point

family: Carrying, dribbling, evasion, and ball progression on foot
justification: carry_episode provides start/end coordinates and lane_occupancy can classify declared frames, but lane traversed over the carry path needs a declared carry-path lane/profile composition.
composition_constraint_note: Requires start/end/path lane alignment semantics for the same carrier and carry interval.

family: Carrying, dribbling, evasion, and ball progression on foot
justification: carry_episode anchors movement-under-control; pressure_on_carrier at carry start/end plus change_across_anchor compares nearest-defender distance across the same carry. Q2 now proves this pressure-reduction composition through reusable binary episode joins in an honest-zero capstone.
composition_constraint_note: Requires pressure measured for the carrier at carry start and carry end under the same carry anchor; Q2 records this alignment explicitly.

family: Carrying, dribbling, evasion, and ball progression on foot
justification: carry_episode anchors the interval and pressure_on_carrier can measure frame-level pressure, but no declared pressure-over-interval/profile aggregation exists.
composition_constraint_note: Requires pressure samples aligned to the carry interval and an aggregation/profile operator.

family: Carrying, dribbling, evasion, and ball progression on foot
justification: carry_episode emits forward_progression_m separately from total displacement, preserving direction-neutral carry semantics while supporting progressive-carry predicates one layer up.

family: Carrying, dribbling, evasion, and ball progression on foot
justification: carry_episode anchors the interval and velocity can measure speed, but no declared speed-profile/trajectory aggregation over a carry interval exists.
composition_constraint_note: Requires velocity samples aligned to the carry interval and an aggregation/profile operator.

family: Carrying, dribbling, evasion, and ball progression on foot
justification: carry_episode can anchor the action, but value gain from a carry requires a learned expected-value model that is outside the current data/runtime modality.
closest_supported_substitute: carry_episode for the observed action only

family: Carrying, dribbling, evasion, and ball progression on foot
justification: carry_episode intentionally claims only observed movement-under-control. Counting defenders bypassed by a carry requires a derived defender_bypassed_by_carry capability with its own endpoint/buffer/domain rules.

family: Carrying, dribbling, evasion, and ball progression on foot
justification: carry_episode and pressure_on_carrier can show a pressured carrier, but a defender engagement/take-on relation needs marking/engagement semantics beyond proximity or pressure.

family: Carrying, dribbling, evasion, and ball progression on foot
justification: carry_episode anchors the on-ball interval and pressure_on_carrier can score pressure; acceleration/deceleration can describe speed change, but direction-change/turn semantics still require a heading-change operator.
closest_supported_substitute: carry_episode + acceleration/deceleration + pressure_on_carrier; heading-change still missing
composition_constraint_note: Requires heading/velocity samples aligned to the carry interval and pressure measured at the change frame.

family: Carrying, dribbling, evasion, and ball progression on foot
justification: carry_episode can anchor a dribble-like movement-under-control, but success as beating a defender needs defender_bypassed_by_carry or a declared take-on outcome primitive.

family: Carrying, dribbling, evasion, and ball progression on foot
justification: carry_episode supports observed progressive carries after the fact, but an available carry option before the action needs a carry-option/space-region generation capability.

family: Carrying, dribbling, evasion, and ball progression on foot
justification: carry_episode anchors the carrier interval and acceleration/deceleration now supports observed stop/start speed changes, but evasion still needs a reviewable direction-change or stop-start composition under pressure.
composition_constraint_note: Requires velocity/acceleration samples and pressure aligned over the carry interval.

family: Dynamic graphs and interaction structure

family: Dynamic graphs and interaction structure
justification: two defenders swapping marks requires marking (assignment) primitive plus its dynamics; neither exists.

family: Dynamic graphs and interaction structure
justification: betweenness centrality requires a constructed passing/option graph; graph construction + centrality computation not built.

family: Dynamic graphs and interaction structure
justification: centrality requires graph construction over interaction edges; not implemented.

family: Dynamic graphs and interaction structure
justification: connected components require a built graph; no graph primitive.

family: Dynamic graphs and interaction structure
justification: a passing connection becoming unavailable: change_across_anchor on geometric_progressive_corridor expresses an edge closing, but a true edge (openness) needs time_to_arrival and a graph frame.

family: Dynamic graphs and interaction structure
justification: a connection opening: change_across_anchor on geometric_progressive_corridor; openness/reachability needs time_to_arrival.

family: Dynamic graphs and interaction structure
justification: defender-to-attacker assignment graph requires marking primitive + graph construction; neither exists.

family: Dynamic graphs and interaction structure
justification: recurring coordinated multi-player movement pattern; needs off_ball_run + motif/graph detection; velocity correlations insufficient.

family: Dynamic graphs and interaction structure
justification: change in passing-network structure over time requires graph construction + temporal comparison; not built.

family: Dynamic graphs and interaction structure
justification: recurring subgraph pattern (e.g. triangles) needs a constructed passing graph + motif matching; pass_chain_episode gives short sequences not graph motifs.

family: Dynamic graphs and interaction structure

family: Dynamic graphs and interaction structure
justification: pressure_on_carrier gives the carrier-defender pressure edge, but a full pressure graph across players needs graph construction over many nodes.

family: Dynamic graphs and interaction structure
justification: pairwise_distance supplies all node-node proximities (edges), but thresholding them into a maintained graph object needs declared graph-construction composition.

family: Dynamic graphs and interaction structure
justification: who-can-reach-whom/space first requires time_to_arrival reachability AND graph construction; both missing.

family: Dynamic graphs and interaction structure

family: Dynamic graphs and interaction structure

family: Dynamic graphs and interaction structure
justification: aggregate structural signature of a team's interaction graph requires constructing the graph + computing graph-level features; not built.

family: Shooting, chance structure, goalmouth defense, and goalkeeper behavior
justification: A backward controlled pass to a teammate (e.g. to GK/back line) is a controlled_pass_episode without forward progression, ordered inside an action_chain. No goal/GK-specific geometry needed to register the backward leg.
closest_supported_substitute: controlled_pass_episode + action_chain (backward direction = no forward progression)

family: Shooting, chance structure, goalmouth defense, and goalkeeper behavior
justification: No shot/goal primitive exists; a 'shot' (ball struck toward goal) cannot be expressed in the catalog, so a blocked shot is unrepresentable.
required_missing_capability: shot_geometry (shot/goal-relative primitive: ball-toward-goal action + block geometry)
closest_supported_substitute: action_event_anchor + pressure_on_carrier (only marks an action under pressure, not a shot or a block)

family: Shooting, chance structure, goalmouth defense, and goalkeeper behavior
justification: Box entry could be approximated via relation_destination_entry into a region, but the terminal 'shot' has no primitive; the sequence cannot resolve its endpoint.
closest_supported_substitute: relation_destination_entry (region entry) + action_chain (entry leg only, not the shot)

family: Shooting, chance structure, goalmouth defense, and goalkeeper behavior
justification: A GK/defender claim zone in the box is goal/box-relative geometry with no catalog primitive; pairwise_distance to a ball cannot delimit a goalmouth claim region.
closest_supported_substitute: pairwise_distance (entity-to-ball) within a region (no goal-frame anchoring)

family: Shooting, chance structure, goalmouth defense, and goalkeeper behavior
justification: The cross (wide lateral delivery) is partly a controlled_pass_episode, but the terminal shot has no primitive, so the cross→shot link is unrepresentable.
required_missing_capability: shot_geometry (shot primitive) to terminate the sequence

family: Shooting, chance structure, goalmouth defense, and goalkeeper behavior
justification: Same as cross_to_shot: the cutback pass is expressible but the shot terminus is not; no shot primitive exists.

family: Shooting, chance structure, goalmouth defense, and goalkeeper behavior
justification: Covering the goal/shot lane is goal-relative defensive geometry; lane_occupancy is explicitly attacking-lane only and there is no goal-frame primitive.

family: Shooting, chance structure, goalmouth defense, and goalkeeper behavior
justification: A GK claiming the ball in the air/box has no GK or goalmouth primitive; identifying the GK also depends on role, and the catch/punch action is not modeled.

family: Shooting, chance structure, goalmouth defense, and goalkeeper behavior
justification: GK depth is GK-position relative to own goal line; no goal-frame primitive and no role taxonomy to pick out the GK.
required_missing_capability: gk_geometry (GK position relative to goal frame)
closest_supported_substitute: relative_position_to_line (generic line, not the goal line)

family: Shooting, chance structure, goalmouth defense, and goalkeeper behavior

required_missing_capability: role_or_reference for GK identity (no role taxonomy)

family: Shooting, chance structure, goalmouth defense, and goalkeeper behavior
justification: Available outlet options can be approximated via support_arrival_relation / open corridors, but enumerating safe reachable options needs time_to_arrival and GK identity needs role.

family: Shooting, chance structure, goalmouth defense, and goalkeeper behavior
justification: How much of the goal the GK covers is goal-frame angular geometry; no goal/GK primitive exists.
closest_supported_substitute: relative_position_to_line (no goal frame, no angular span)

family: Shooting, chance structure, goalmouth defense, and goalkeeper behavior
justification: A race-to-ball comparison is the time_to_arrival pattern (like the intervention/interception margin); velocity + pairwise_distance give raw inputs but reachability time is not a primitive, and GK identity needs role.

family: Shooting, chance structure, goalmouth defense, and goalkeeper behavior
justification: GK left/right alignment relative to ball-and-goal is goal-frame geometry; ball_lateral_fraction is pitch-width, not goal-relative, and no GK primitive exists.

family: Shooting, chance structure, goalmouth defense, and goalkeeper behavior
justification: Depends on both a shot primitive (the anchor moment) and goal-frame GK geometry; neither exists.

family: Shooting, chance structure, goalmouth defense, and goalkeeper behavior
justification: Punch vs catch is a GK aerial-action distinction with no primitive; also benefits from body/contact detail beyond tracking.

family: Shooting, chance structure, goalmouth defense, and goalkeeper behavior

family: Shooting, chance structure, goalmouth defense, and goalkeeper behavior

family: Shooting, chance structure, goalmouth defense, and goalkeeper behavior

family: Shooting, chance structure, goalmouth defense, and goalkeeper behavior
justification: Post-shot xG is a learned probabilistic model; learned models are explicitly not implemented.

family: Shooting, chance structure, goalmouth defense, and goalkeeper behavior
justification: A rebound presupposes a shot event (off keeper/post); without a shot primitive the originating event and its loose-ball continuation cannot be anchored.
required_missing_capability: shot_geometry (shot primitive to anchor the rebound)

family: Shooting, chance structure, goalmouth defense, and goalkeeper behavior
justification: Requires both a shot primitive and a GK save action; neither exists in the catalog.

family: Shooting, chance structure, goalmouth defense, and goalkeeper behavior

family: Shooting, chance structure, goalmouth defense, and goalkeeper behavior
justification: Purely geometric goal angle, but there is no goal-frame primitive in the catalog (per calibration, shot geometry is missing_primitive shot_geometry, not supported).

family: Shooting, chance structure, goalmouth defense, and goalkeeper behavior

family: Shooting, chance structure, goalmouth defense, and goalkeeper behavior
justification: Distance to goal is geometric but requires a goal reference point that no primitive provides (calibration: shot geometry = missing_primitive).

family: Shooting, chance structure, goalmouth defense, and goalkeeper behavior
justification: xG is a learned probabilistic model, explicitly not implemented.

family: Shooting, chance structure, goalmouth defense, and goalkeeper behavior
justification: A clear lane from shooter to goal is goal-frame geometry plus defender occlusion; no goal primitive and lane_occupancy is attacking-lane only, not a shot lane.

family: Shooting, chance structure, goalmouth defense, and goalkeeper behavior

family: Shooting, chance structure, goalmouth defense, and goalkeeper behavior

family: Shooting, chance structure, goalmouth defense, and goalkeeper behavior

family: Shooting, chance structure, goalmouth defense, and goalkeeper behavior

family: Shooting, chance structure, goalmouth defense, and goalkeeper behavior

family: Possession behavior, circulation, combinations, and repeated patterns

family: Possession behavior, circulation, combinations, and repeated patterns

justification: Rate of ball circulation = pass cadence within a possession_segment, the pass_tempo/touch cadence over an action_chain.

family: Possession behavior, circulation, combinations, and repeated patterns

family: Possession behavior, circulation, combinations, and repeated patterns

family: Possession behavior, circulation, combinations, and repeated patterns

family: Possession behavior, circulation, combinations, and repeated patterns

family: Possession behavior, circulation, combinations, and repeated patterns
justification: Individual patterns (action_chains) are detectable and countable via count_at_least, but matching recurrence of a pattern signature requires a declared similarity/match rule over chains.

family: Possession behavior, circulation, combinations, and repeated patterns
justification: Possession speeding up = rising progression rate / ball velocity across a possession_segment, captured via change_across_anchor on velocity + progression.

family: Possession behavior, circulation, combinations, and repeated patterns
justification: Mirror of acceleration: falling progression/velocity across the possession_segment via change_across_anchor.

family: Possession behavior, circulation, combinations, and repeated patterns
justification: How deep/forward possession is held maps to team_compactness depth and ball lateral/longitudinal placement over the possession_segment.

family: Possession behavior, circulation, combinations, and repeated patterns
justification: Directness = forward progression vs lateral/back over a possession_segment; per calibration, directness is supported via possession_segment + controlled_pass_episode + action_chain.

family: Possession behavior, circulation, combinations, and repeated patterns
justification: Net forward distance gained across a possession = change_across_anchor on ball position over the possession_segment.

family: Possession behavior, circulation, combinations, and repeated patterns
justification: Length of a possession_segment, measured with persists_for. Direct.

family: Possession behavior, circulation, combinations, and repeated patterns
justification: Forward distance per time over a possession_segment = change_across_anchor on position divided by persists_for / velocity.

family: Possession behavior, circulation, combinations, and repeated patterns
justification: Retention/safety of possession = controlled_pass_episode completion proportion + low pressure_on_carrier across the possession_segment (retention is a named catalog strength).

family: Possession behavior, circulation, combinations, and repeated patterns
justification: A 'signature' of a possession is just a name; it could be any aggregate of supported metrics but its features/identity criteria are unspecified.

family: Possession behavior, circulation, combinations, and repeated patterns
justification: Stalled possession = sustained low/no forward progression with passing persisting (persists_for) and low velocity across a possession_segment.

family: Possession behavior, circulation, combinations, and repeated patterns
justification: Lateral spread of possession = team_compactness width and ball_lateral_fraction range over the possession_segment.

family: Possession behavior, circulation, combinations, and repeated patterns
justification: Recycling = backward/lateral controlled passes to restart attack within a possession_segment, an action_chain without net forward progression.

family: Possession behavior, circulation, combinations, and repeated patterns

family: Possession behavior, circulation, combinations, and repeated patterns
justification: Circulating possession down one flank = possession_segment of controlled passes with sustained high ball_lateral_fraction on one side.

family: Possession behavior, circulation, combinations, and repeated patterns
justification: Count of switches over a window = count_at_least over switch_of_play events.

family: Possession behavior, circulation, combinations, and repeated patterns
justification: A switch and its surrounding legs = switch_of_play anchored in an action_chain / pass_chain_episode.

family: Possession behavior, circulation, combinations, and repeated patterns
justification: Where on the pitch possession is held = possession_segment with ball position/team_compactness aggregated by pitch third; territory is an aggregate of supported geometry.

family: Possession behavior, circulation, combinations, and repeated patterns
justification: A->B->C combination with B as a relay is a one_touch_relay_episode chained into a pass_chain_episode over three players, same-possession ordered.

family: Possession behavior, circulation, combinations, and repeated patterns
justification: Touches-per-action cadence over a possession_segment via controlled_pass_episode/one_touch_relay_episode counts and persists_for.

family: Possession behavior, circulation, combinations, and repeated patterns
justification: Up-back-through is a three-leg ordered pattern: forward controlled_pass, backward lay-off (one_touch_relay_episode), then a progressive corridor pass; all same-possession in action_chain.

family: Possession behavior, circulation, combinations, and repeated patterns
justification: Wall pass = one-two; one_touch_relay_episode with the ball returned first-time, ordered in an action_chain (synonym of give_and_go).

family: Possession behavior, circulation, combinations, and repeated patterns
justification: Forward progression down a flank = pass_chain_episode of forward controlled passes with high ball_lateral_fraction.

family: Passing lanes, windows, reception, and distribution effects
justification: Ball entering a target region within a window is directly relation_destination_entry (outcome-window).

family: Passing lanes, windows, reception, and distribution effects
justification: Forward progression of the ball/pass is a core attribute of controlled_pass_episode and geometric_progressive_corridor.

family: Passing lanes, windows, reception, and distribution effects
justification: A sideways ball transfer = controlled_pass_episode with lateral ball_lateral_fraction change and no forward progression; large ones are switch_of_play.

family: Passing lanes, windows, reception, and distribution effects
justification: A first-time onward distribution is a one_touch_relay_episode within an action_chain.

family: Passing lanes, windows, reception, and distribution effects
justification: Directly the opponents_bypassed_by_action primitive (bypass count).

family: Passing lanes, windows, reception, and distribution effects
justification: Pass completion probability is a learned model, explicitly not implemented.

family: Passing lanes, windows, reception, and distribution effects
justification: How disruptive/risky a pass is to defenders requires reachability/interception margin of nearby defenders to the ball path; that is the time_to_arrival gap, not a learned score per se.

family: Passing lanes, windows, reception, and distribution effects
justification: An open forward corridor ball->teammate is supported via geometric_progressive_corridor; but a general pass lane as a defensible/interceptable channel needs time_to_arrival and lane-as-defense, which are missing.

family: Passing lanes, windows, reception, and distribution effects
justification: Whether the lane is clear of intercepting defenders is reachability/occlusion, not a geometric corridor alone; needs time_to_arrival and cover_shadow.

family: Passing lanes, windows, reception, and distribution effects
justification: Interception margin = defender time-to-ball-line vs ball-arrival time. This is precisely the time_to_arrival primitive the catalog lacks.

family: Passing lanes, windows, reception, and distribution effects
justification: Geometric distance of the pass = pairwise_distance over the pass leg of a controlled_pass_episode.

family: Passing lanes, windows, reception, and distribution effects
justification: Probability a player selects a given pass is a learned/probabilistic model, not implemented.

family: Passing lanes, windows, reception, and distribution effects
justification: Ball speed across the pass = velocity (displacement-derived) over the controlled_pass_episode leg.

family: Passing lanes, windows, reception, and distribution effects
justification: Direction + magnitude of the pass = velocity vector + pairwise_distance over the controlled_pass_episode; signed lateral component via ball_lateral_fraction.

family: Passing lanes, windows, reception, and distribution effects
justification: A passing window narrowing over time is defender reachability shrinking the safe lane; this is the time_to_arrival gap (window = reachability margin over time).
closest_supported_substitute: pairwise_distance + velocity (raw closing of geometry, not window safety)

family: Passing lanes, windows, reception, and distribution effects
justification: How long a window stays open is persists_for applied to a window state that itself needs time_to_arrival to define.
closest_supported_substitute: persists_for (over a window state that doesn't yet exist)

family: Passing lanes, windows, reception, and distribution effects
justification: An episode where a safe pass window exists requires the window/reachability concept (time_to_arrival); the open-forward-corridor subset is supported but the general safe window is not.

family: Passing lanes, windows, reception, and distribution effects
justification: A window opening = reachability margin growing; needs time_to_arrival to define the window state being entered.

family: Passing lanes, windows, reception, and distribution effects
justification: Rate of window opening is the time-derivative of an undefined window state; depends on the missing time_to_arrival primitive.

family: Passing lanes, windows, reception, and distribution effects
justification: Open/closed window state is a reachability-margin classification needing time_to_arrival; no such primitive exists.

family: Passing lanes, windows, reception, and distribution effects
justification: Reception then forward progression by the receiver is detectable via support_arrival_relation + subsequent forward action, but whether the player can/did turn is fundamentally body-orientation, an unavailable modality.

family: Passing lanes, windows, reception, and distribution effects
justification: Receiving beyond a line/unit is controlled_line_break_episode + receiver_line_transition_during_pass_leg against a multi_line_model rank; receiver-beyond-line is calibrated supported.

family: Passing lanes, windows, reception, and distribution effects
justification: Receiving between two ranked lines = relative_position_to_line against two adjacent ranks of multi_line_model (rank-N honesty handles missing lines), with support_arrival_relation for the receipt.

family: Passing lanes, windows, reception, and distribution effects
justification: Back-foot receipt is a body-orientation/stance distinction; tracking position cannot determine which foot/orientation faced play. Body orientation is unavailable.

family: Passing lanes, windows, reception, and distribution effects

family: Passing lanes, windows, reception, and distribution effects
justification: Receipt with a close, closing defender = support_arrival_relation + pressure_on_carrier on the receiver; same pattern as the calibrated throw-in-under-pressure case.

family: Passing lanes, windows, reception, and distribution effects
justification: Margin between receiver arriving at the ball and a defender = receiver vs defender time-to-ball; this is the time_to_arrival reachability primitive.

family: Passing lanes, windows, reception, and distribution effects
justification: Time/space the receiver has to control before pressure arrives = reachability of the nearest defender to the receipt point; the time_to_arrival gap.

family: Passing lanes, windows, reception, and distribution effects
justification: Per calibration, receiver openness is mostly missing_primitive (time_to_arrival/reachability + lane-as-defense); proximity alone does not capture how open a receiver is to be reached safely.

family: Passing lanes, windows, reception, and distribution effects
justification: Where the receipt occurs relative to regions/lines = receiving_location relative to relative_position_to_line and region anchors; covered by support_arrival_relation + relative_position_to_line.

family: Passing lanes, windows, reception, and distribution effects
justification: How a pass shifts the defensive structure = change_across_anchor on team_compactness / signed_lateral_shift / defensive_outfield_centroid across the pass anchor.

family: Passing lanes, windows, reception, and distribution effects
justification: Ball reaching a specific target player in a region is relation_destination_entry + support_arrival_relation; pinning it to a named/role-specific 'target player' needs reference/role resolution.

family: Entities, availability, roles, and tactical identity
justification: Which players are on-pitch over time is registered_entity presence under tracking_quality coverage; basic availability state is supported.

family: Entities, availability, roles, and tactical identity
justification: A sending-off is an event-marked anchor (red card) via action_event_anchor with a player leaving the registered_entity active set thereafter.

family: Entities, availability, roles, and tactical identity
justification: Reconciling provider IDs/aliases to a single entity is an identity/reference resolution that the registered_entity layer provides (basic availability/identity, not role).

family: Entities, availability, roles, and tactical identity
justification: Whether an entity is currently tracked/visible is exactly tracking_quality (coverage/UNKNOWN when thin) over a registered_entity.

family: Entities, availability, roles, and tactical identity
justification: The set of entities expected on pitch (e.g. 11 + GK) is a reference set over registered_entity / team_membership, checkable with count_at_least and exists.

family: Entities, availability, roles, and tactical identity
justification: A timeline of who is the GK requires identifying the goalkeeper role; there is no role taxonomy (known gap), so GK designation cannot be resolved.

family: Entities, availability, roles, and tactical identity
justification: A provider-supplied position label is a role/position taxonomy attribute; the catalog has no role taxonomy and does not ingest position labels as a primitive.

family: Entities, availability, roles, and tactical identity
justification: A named set of reference players is an entity set over registered_entity / team_membership; resolvable when defined by identity rather than role.

family: Entities, availability, roles, and tactical identity
justification: Directly the registered_entity primitive (catalog entity identity).

family: Entities, availability, roles, and tactical identity
justification: Detecting a change of a player's role/position requires a role taxonomy to change between; no role taxonomy exists.
closest_supported_substitute: change_across_anchor on position (positional shift, not a role change)

family: Entities, availability, roles, and tactical identity
justification: Two players swapping roles needs role identities to swap; no role taxonomy. Positional swap is observable but role attribution is not.

family: Entities, availability, roles, and tactical identity
justification: A substitution is an event-marked anchor with one registered_entity leaving and another entering the active set; availability change is supported.

family: Entities, availability, roles, and tactical identity
justification: Assigning a tactical role to a player is precisely the missing role taxonomy (known gap).

family: Entities, availability, roles, and tactical identity
justification: A timespan during which a player holds a tactical role depends on role assignment, which the catalog lacks.
closest_supported_substitute: persists_for over position zones (no role labeling)

family: Entities, availability, roles, and tactical identity
justification: Which team an entity belongs to is the team_membership relation in the catalog; basic identity, not role.

family: Entities, availability, roles, and tactical identity
justification: Membership in a tactical unit (defence/midfield/attack) presupposes a role/unit taxonomy; line-rank membership via multi_line_model is observable but does not equal a named unit. Mostly role gap.

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: Supported as observed pressure_on_carrier after a loss/regain transition: transition_anchor supplies the phase hinge and pressure_on_carrier supplies distance, closing speed, and approach angle from behind. No pressure quality or defender intent is inferred.
composition_constraint_note: Pressure frame/window must be aligned after the relevant loss/regain transition for the same perspective team.

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: Defensive block compactness is team_compactness (width/depth/dispersion) computed on the defensive outfield set, with defensive_outfield_centroid as the reference.

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: Depth of the defensive block is the depth component of team_compactness measured over the defensive outfield set.

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: Lateral movement of the block is signed_lateral_shift (block shift) over defensive_outfield_centroid, episodized with persists_for.

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: pressure_on_carrier and time_to_arrival can describe defender proximity/reachability while the carrier is slowed or held up, but a containment episode still needs a frozen operational definition for containment vs ordinary pressure.

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: time_to_arrival now gives fixed-point reachability, but a cover relation behind a pressing defender requires cover-shadow/lane-coverage geometry and cover reference semantics.

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: Cover shadow / passing-lane denial behind a defender is exactly the missing reachability geometry; no cover_shadow or time_to_arrival primitive exists.
closest_supported_substitute: lane_occupancy (attacking lane occupation only, not defensive shadow)

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: Cross-lane blocking requires reachability to a lane or ball path, not just arrival at one static point. Static time_to_arrival is implemented; lane/path interception remains missing.

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: Supported as static-point time_to_arrival: defender/candidate straight-line arrival time to a declared target point under a frozen max-speed assumption, with coverage status and point-mass limitation carried as evidence.

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: time_to_arrival can evaluate defender reachability to a receiver point, but access before ball arrival needs dynamic pass-flight/threshold alignment not yet modeled as a generic composition constraint.

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: Local defensive numerical advantage is local_number_relation (local counts/overloads) evaluated for the defending team in a region.

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: Supported for observed post-pass defensive reachability change: action_event_anchor/change_across_anchor align before/after pass frames and time_to_arrival measures fixed-point reachability. Does not infer intent or tactical causation.

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: Pressure duration, closing speed, and time_to_arrival can support an observed delay proxy, but the episode definition needs a frozen threshold separating delay from ordinary pressure.

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: Direct pressure on the ball carrier is exactly pressure_on_carrier (nearest-defender distance + closing_speed + approach_angle + duration).

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: Pressure approach_angle (from pressure_on_carrier) shows the side pressure comes from, and the carrier's resulting velocity/heading shows direction taken, but asserting the defender 'forced' a direction (denied the other side) needs cover_shadow/reachability.

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: Pressure exerted without being the nearest defender (covering a lane/option rather than the ball) is reachability/shadow-based; pressure_on_carrier is single nearest-defender only.

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: A defender attempting/intercepting a pass requires reachability to a moving ball or pass line during the flight window. Static-point time_to_arrival is insufficient and no intent is inferred.

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: Assigning a defender to mark an attacker is the marking primitive; pairwise_distance gives nearest-opponent distance but no marking-assignment primitive exists.

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: Distance from a defender to the attacker being marked is pairwise_distance, but treating it as 'marking' proximity (which attacker is the assignment) needs the marking primitive.

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: Per calibration, marking tightness/man-marking = missing_primitive (marking); pairwise_distance gives nearest-opponent distance but no marking primitive.

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: Pressure from multiple defenders at multiple angles is a team/multi-actor press primitive; pressure_on_carrier is single-carrier (single nearest defender) only.

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: Nearest defender to a target is the nearest-defender component already inside pressure_on_carrier, and directly via pairwise_distance min over opponents.

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: Passing-lane denial requires lane/path coverage or cover-shadow geometry. Static time_to_arrival closes endpoint reachability only, not lane denial.

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: Per calibration, press onset / closing down = SUPPORTED via pressure_on_carrier (closing_speed rising + distance falling marks onset).

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: End of a press is the falling/clearing edge of pressure_on_carrier (distance growing, closing_speed dropping below threshold); the same single-carrier primitive bounds it.

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: Per calibration, press trap / pressing trigger = missing_primitive (team-level press primitive); pressure_on_carrier is single-carrier only and cannot express a collective trap.

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: A single-carrier pressing episode is pressure_on_carrier sustained via persists_for; SUPPORTED for the on-ball pressing actor.

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: The set of defenders applying pressure is a multi-actor/team press construct; pressure_on_carrier resolves only the single nearest defender, not a pressure actor set.

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: Direction pressure is applied from is the approach_angle component of pressure_on_carrier.

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: Diversity of pressure (multiple defenders from multiple angles) is a team/multi-actor press property; pressure_on_carrier is single-carrier only.

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: Per calibration, pressure intensity = SUPPORTED via pressure_on_carrier (distance + closing_speed + approach_angle + duration give intensity).

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: transition_anchor can identify the post-loss recovery phase and velocity gives displacement direction, but a typed off_ball_run/recovery-run episode is still missing. Velocity alone is a measurement, not a run episode.

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: A defender blocking the shot lane to goal is defensive lane/shadow coverage requiring reachability geometry; lane_occupancy is attacking only.

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: carry_episode anchors the carrier and pressure_on_carrier can show defensive proximity, but confirming a tackle attempt needs contact/tackle event evidence or a contest primitive.

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: Per calibration, collective press synchrony / team press shape = missing_primitive (team-level press primitive); pressure_on_carrier is single-carrier only.

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: Carrier pinned near the touchline can be derived from ball_lateral_fraction (near pitch edge) plus pressure_on_carrier, but 'isolation' (denial of inside options) requires reachability/shadow of escape lanes.

family: Pressure, marking, cover, denial, and collective defending
justification: A defender tracking an attacker's run is a marking/man-tracking relation over time; pairwise_distance shows co-movement distance but no marking primitive asserts the tracking assignment.

family: Transitions, counterpressure, recovery, and rest defense
justification: Supported by transition_anchor for the regain hinge plus outcome_window for the bounded post-regain window. This claims only an observed possession-role transition and declared window, not counterattack quality.

family: Transitions, counterpressure, recovery, and rest defense
justification: transition_anchor supplies loss/regain anchors, but a reusable duration relation pairing a loss transition to a later regain transition is not yet declared as an authorable capability.
composition_constraint_note: Must pair a possession_loss_anchor with the next compatible possession_gain_anchor for the same team perspective.

family: Transitions, counterpressure, recovery, and rest defense
justification: Supported as a declared candidate using transition_anchor for regain, action_chain / progressive-corridor or velocity substrate for fast forward progression, and outcome_window for the post-regain window. Does not claim counterattack quality or intent.
composition_constraint_note: Fast-progression window must share the same regain anchor and team perspective.

family: Transitions, counterpressure, recovery, and rest defense
justification: transition_anchor scopes the loss/regain hinge and time_to_arrival can measure fixed-point reachability after it, but preventing a counterattack requires outlet/cover-shadow semantics not yet implemented.

family: Transitions, counterpressure, recovery, and rest defense
justification: transition_anchor can scope the post-loss window and pressure_on_carrier can measure one carrier-pressure relation, but a counterpress actor set requires a multi-actor pressure/team-press capability.

family: Transitions, counterpressure, recovery, and rest defense
justification: Supported as pressure_on_carrier aligned after a possession-loss transition_anchor. This claims observed pressure components after loss, not coordinated counterpress quality or intent.
composition_constraint_note: Pressure window must be after the loss transition and use the same team perspective.

family: Transitions, counterpressure, recovery, and rest defense
justification: transition_anchor can start the post-loss defensive window and team_compactness can measure shape, but the criterion for reorganization/settled shape is not yet a declared thresholded capability.
composition_constraint_note: Shape measurements must align to the post-loss window for the defending team perspective.

family: Transitions, counterpressure, recovery, and rest defense
justification: Supported by transition_anchor for the loss hinge plus outcome_window for the bounded post-loss window. This claims only an observed transition window, not defensive recovery quality.

family: Transitions, counterpressure, recovery, and rest defense
justification: Supported by transition_anchor for the regain hinge and action_chain for the first same-possession action after that anchor.
composition_constraint_note: The action-chain start must align to the regain anchor and must not bridge a turnover/stoppage.

family: Transitions, counterpressure, recovery, and rest defense
justification: Supported by transition_anchor for the regain hinge, action_chain for the first action, and progressive-corridor/forward-progression predicates for the forward-action condition.
composition_constraint_note: The action and progression predicate must share the regain anchor and team perspective.

family: Transitions, counterpressure, recovery, and rest defense
justification: Supported by possession-loss transition_anchor with anchor frame and ball/location evidence. This claims the observed loss anchor location only.

family: Transitions, counterpressure, recovery, and rest defense
justification: transition_anchor plus corridor and fixed-point reachability can score individual outlet candidates, but assembling a typed opposition outlet set/graph remains missing.

family: Transitions, counterpressure, recovery, and rest defense
justification: transition_anchor and time_to_arrival can evaluate declared outlet points, but denial over a set of outlets requires option-graph and/or cover-shadow semantics.

family: Transitions, counterpressure, recovery, and rest defense
justification: Supported directly by transition_anchor when possession role changes into the perspective team. Emits typed gain/regain anchor evidence rather than a plain segment boundary.

family: Transitions, counterpressure, recovery, and rest defense
justification: Supported directly by transition_anchor when possession role changes away from the perspective team. Emits typed loss anchor evidence rather than a plain segment boundary.

family: Transitions, counterpressure, recovery, and rest defense
justification: Supported by transition_anchor for the regain and outcome_window for retained, ball-alive possession over a declared post-regain duration.
composition_constraint_note: Outcome window must start at the same regain anchor and preserve the same team perspective.

family: Transitions, counterpressure, recovery, and rest defense
justification: Supported as team_compactness / shape measurement inside a defensive-transition window anchored by transition_anchor. Claims observed shape state only, not recovery quality.
composition_constraint_note: Shape frame/window must align after a loss transition for the defending team perspective.

family: Transitions, counterpressure, recovery, and rest defense
justification: Supported by possession-gain transition_anchor with anchor frame and location evidence. This claims the observed regain anchor location only.

family: Transitions, counterpressure, recovery, and rest defense
justification: transition/possession substrate exists, and team_compactness can measure shape, but rest-attack structure requires a declared definition of the in-possession high/covering subset.
closest_supported_substitute: possession/transition substrate + team_compactness over a declared subset
composition_constraint_note: Needs a declared entity subset/zone rule for rest-attack actors.

family: Transitions, counterpressure, recovery, and rest defense
justification: transition/possession substrate exists, and local_number/team_compactness can measure deep cover, but rest-defense balance requires a declared rest-defense subset and threshold policy.
composition_constraint_note: Needs a declared entity subset/zone rule for rest-defense actors.

family: Transitions, counterpressure, recovery, and rest defense
justification: Counting deep players is supported with local_number_relation, but calling them rest-defense requires a declared rest-defense subset/zone definition.
composition_constraint_note: Needs a declared entity subset/zone rule for rest-defense actors.

family: Transitions, counterpressure, recovery, and rest defense
justification: Depth can be measured with team_compactness/line geometry, but rest-defense depth requires a declared rest-defense subset/zone definition.
closest_supported_substitute: team_compactness depth over declared deep subset
composition_constraint_note: Needs a declared entity subset/zone rule for rest-defense actors.

family: Transitions, counterpressure, recovery, and rest defense
justification: Width can be measured with team_compactness, but rest-defense width requires a declared rest-defense subset/zone definition.
closest_supported_substitute: team_compactness width over declared deep subset
composition_constraint_note: Needs a declared entity subset/zone rule for rest-defense actors.

family: Transitions, counterpressure, recovery, and rest defense
justification: transition_anchor scopes the transition and time_to_arrival supports fixed-point reachability, but open-space regions/fields are not generated yet.

family: Transitions, counterpressure, recovery, and rest defense
justification: Supported by transition_anchor for the transition moment/window and local_number_relation for observed numerical difference around the declared reference region.
composition_constraint_note: Local-number measurement must align to the transition anchor/window and team perspective.

family: Transitions, counterpressure, recovery, and rest defense
justification: transition_anchor and outcome_window support declared fixed windows, but an endogenous transition-termination condition needs a reviewable definition of what ends the transition.

family: Reachability, interception, space control, and spatial value
justification: Single target-point time_to_arrival exists; a field of arrival times over many pitch locations still needs field/grid generation.

family: Reachability, interception, space control, and spatial value
justification: time_to_arrival can classify whether a declared point is reachable within a threshold; an available-space episode over a region still needs region/field generation.

family: Reachability, interception, space control, and spatial value
justification: Static-point time_to_arrival exists, but a region of available/free space requires generating and aggregating reachable pitch regions.

family: Reachability, interception, space control, and spatial value
justification: Value of space being closed is a learned space-value model; per calibration learned space-value = missing_modality (learned-models).

family: Reachability, interception, space control, and spatial value
justification: A probabilistic pitch-control field is a learned/probabilistic model; per calibration pitch control = learned/model = missing_modality.

family: Reachability, interception, space control, and spatial value
justification: Controlled area requires a reachability/Voronoi-style region. The implemented primitive evaluates arrival to declared target points only.

family: Reachability, interception, space control, and spatial value
justification: Dominant control region requires comparing arrival times across a generated spatial field; static target-point time_to_arrival is only an input.

family: Reachability, interception, space control, and spatial value
justification: A free-space field requires a grid/field of reachability values. Static-point time_to_arrival is implemented but not field generation.

family: Reachability, interception, space control, and spatial value
justification: Value of space generated is a learned space-value model; per calibration learned space-value = missing_modality.

family: Reachability, interception, space control, and spatial value
justification: Interception margin compares defender arrival to a moving ball/pass line and ball timing. Static-point time_to_arrival is implemented but does not model ball-path interception.

family: Reachability, interception, space control, and spatial value
justification: time_to_arrival can produce attacker/defender arrival times to the same declared point, but a generic margin/difference operator for comparing those times is not yet available.

family: Reachability, interception, space control, and spatial value
justification: Value of occupied space is a learned space-value model; per calibration learned space-value = missing_modality.

family: Reachability, interception, space control, and spatial value
justification: Per calibration, pitch control = learned/model = missing_modality (learned-models).

family: Reachability, interception, space control, and spatial value
justification: Player reachability to one declared point is supported; the full region reachable inside a horizon requires region generation.

family: Reachability, interception, space control, and spatial value
justification: Accessibility of a declared target point is supported via time_to_arrival; accessibility of a region/space remains a region-generation gap.

family: Reachability, interception, space control, and spatial value
justification: time_to_arrival supports fixed target points, but a closing rate for space requires repeated field/region measures and a rate operator.

family: Reachability, interception, space control, and spatial value
justification: time_to_arrival can test declared points before/after an anchor, but detecting created space as a region still needs region generation and composition constraints.

family: Reachability, interception, space control, and spatial value
justification: time_to_arrival can evaluate declared target-point reachability; denying space generally requires cover-shadow or generated reachability regions.

family: Reachability, interception, space control, and spatial value
justification: carry_episode can express one observed way to enter space, but exploitation still needs generated free-space regions and/or off-ball-run action typing for non-carriers.

family: Reachability, interception, space control, and spatial value
justification: time_to_arrival supports fixed target-point reachability, but opening rate requires a repeated space/field measure and rate operator.

family: Reachability, interception, space control, and spatial value
justification: Space transfer between zones requires generated reachability fields across zones; static point arrival and structured zones are inputs only.

family: Reachability, interception, space control, and spatial value
justification: Team reachability to declared points can be evaluated, but producing a team reachability region requires field/region generation.

family: Reachability, interception, space control, and spatial value
justification: time_to_arrival supports a static target point. Intercepting the moving ball requires moving-target/ball-path interception.

family: Reachability, interception, space control, and spatial value
justification: Supported directly: time_to_arrival estimates observed candidate arrival time to a declared static pitch location under straight-line max-speed, with tracking coverage and point-mass limitation carried.

family: Reachability, interception, space control, and spatial value
justification: Moving-target arrival/interception remains missing; v0.1 time_to_arrival is explicitly static-point.

family: Data quality, synchronization, and provenance
justification: Quality/confidence of the ball track maps to tracking_quality (UNKNOWN when thin) applied to the ball entity.

family: Data quality, synchronization, and provenance
justification: Estimating drift between event clock and tracking clock is a sync primitive; tracking_quality covers presence/coverage but not a clock-drift model. Whether 'drift' here means a detected misalignment flag or a fitted offset model is undefined.

family: Data quality, synchronization, and provenance
justification: A fixed event-vs-tracking clock offset estimate is a sync primitive not covered by tracking_quality; per guidance clock-offset/sync may be supported or needs_definition and the intended representation (flag vs fitted scalar) is undefined.

family: Data quality, synchronization, and provenance
justification: Flagging implausible position coordinates is a tracking_quality signal (positions degraded/UNKNOWN when implausible/thin).

family: Data quality, synchronization, and provenance
justification: Aligning an event to a tracking frame is event-tracking sync; action_event_anchor anchors events to time but there is no explicit alignment-confidence primitive, and 'candidate alignment' representation is undefined (sync, not core coverage).

family: Data quality, synchronization, and provenance
justification: Confidence in event-to-tracking alignment is a sync-quality measure; tracking_quality covers tracking presence but not event-alignment confidence, and the exact metric is undefined.

family: Data quality, synchronization, and provenance
justification: Detecting that two players' track identities were swapped is an entity identity/reference problem; there is no player identity-resolution or role/reference primitive (player ROLE taxonomy is a stated gap), and tracking_quality flags thinness not identity confusion.

family: Data quality, synchronization, and provenance
justification: A mask of which samples were interpolated/filled is part of tracking_quality provenance (positions known vs UNKNOWN/derived when thin).

family: Data quality, synchronization, and provenance
justification: Irregular/variable sampling cadence is a tracking_quality condition (drives UNKNOWN/sparse handling, e.g. velocity UNKNOWN when sparse).

family: Data quality, synchronization, and provenance
justification: Implausible velocity/displacement jumps are detectable from velocity (displacement-derived) combined with tracking_quality flagging the segment UNKNOWN/degraded.

family: Data quality, synchronization, and provenance
justification: Per-sample measurement uncertainty is what tracking_quality expresses (confidence, UNKNOWN when thin).

family: Data quality, synchronization, and provenance
justification: Quality of pitch coordinate calibration is a provenance/calibration measure; tracking_quality covers entity-track presence but not camera/pitch homography calibration quality, and the intended scope is undefined.

family: Data quality, synchronization, and provenance
justification: Disagreement between event providers is a multi-source provenance/conflict check; the catalog has no cross-provider comparison primitive and the comparison semantics are undefined (events available but no provider-conflict operator).

family: Data quality, synchronization, and provenance
justification: A histogram of why quality is degraded aggregates tracking_quality reason codes via count; tracking_quality supplies the reasons and count is an available operator.

family: Data quality, synchronization, and provenance
justification: Whether all required entities are tracked is tracking_quality presence/coverage with count_at_least over expected entities; per guidance coverage/presence is SUPPORTED.

family: Data quality, synchronization, and provenance
justification: An episode of missing samples is a tracking_quality gap (UNKNOWN when thin) bounded via persists_for.

family: Data quality, synchronization, and provenance
justification: Provenance of smoothing/filtering applied to tracks is part of the tracking_quality provenance record (how positions were derived).

family: Data quality, synchronization, and provenance
justification: Which source produced a measurement is provenance carried by tracking_quality (and per-result provenance of positions/events).

family: Data quality, synchronization, and provenance
justification: Fraction of time/entities tracked is a tracking_quality coverage ratio (rate/count over present samples); per guidance coverage is SUPPORTED.

family: Data quality, synchronization, and provenance
justification: Whether an entity is present/tracked at a time is exactly tracking_quality presence (UNKNOWN when absent/thin); per guidance presence is SUPPORTED.

family: Restarts and set-piece behavior
justification: set_piece_structure now exposes the corner restart and observed at-frame arrangement, but typing a corner routine/variant (in-swing, short, near-post) still requires delivery/run-pattern recognition.

family: Restarts and set-piece behavior
justification: controlled_pass_episode + set_piece_structure can anchor a restart pass/cross, but flagging it as a delivery with target/trajectory semantics still needs delivery typing beyond observed restart arrangement.

family: Restarts and set-piece behavior
justification: Where the delivery lands is observable via relation_destination_entry into a region; but the region must be a structured set-piece target zone (near post / 6-yard / penalty spot) which needs structured_zones / set_piece_structure.

family: Restarts and set-piece behavior
justification: set_piece_structure can anchor the restart, but classifying delivery flight (in-swing/out-swing/driven/floated) requires curved-trajectory typing of ball path; no trajectory-class primitive exists, ground/air not modeled at this granularity.

family: Restarts and set-piece behavior
justification: First touch after a delivery is detectable as the next action_event_anchor/controlled_pass touch following the restart; but identifying it as the set-piece 'first contact' needs the restart-to-contact composition and a touch/contact primitive distinct from a completed pass.

family: Restarts and set-piece behavior
justification: set_piece_structure can anchor the restart, but determining WHO wins the aerial/ground first contact is a contested-ball/duel outcome; no duel or contest primitive exists, and air contests need height the model does not resolve.

family: Restarts and set-piece behavior
justification: set_piece_structure now exposes the free-kick restart and observed at-frame arrangement, but typing direct-shot / wall-pass / worked free-kick routines still requires routine-pattern recognition.

family: Restarts and set-piece behavior
justification: set_piece_structure exposes the goal-kick restart and at-frame arrangement, but goal-kick build structure requires build-pattern evolution after the restart plus role/reference grouping.

family: Restarts and set-piece behavior
justification: set_piece_structure can expose the penalty event and observed arrangement, but penalty setup state needs role/reference identification of taker, goalkeeper, and encroachment structure.

family: Restarts and set-piece behavior
justification: The moment a restart is awarded is an event-marked anchor; action_event_anchor (incl. event-marked restarts) handles it directly.

family: Restarts and set-piece behavior
justification: The moment the restart is taken is an event-marked restart anchor handled by action_event_anchor (matches throw-in/restart calibration).

family: Restarts and set-piece behavior
justification: The restart category (throw-in/corner/free kick/goal kick/kickoff) comes from the event label on the restart anchor; action_event_anchor with outcome_classification reads it directly.

family: Restarts and set-piece behavior
justification: set_piece_structure can provide restart context, but screening/blocking runs are marking-interference patterns requiring blocker/marker roles and run interaction typing.

family: Restarts and set-piece behavior
justification: Recovery of a loose ball after first contact is a possession-change to a recovering team, observable via change_across_anchor + possession_segment, but the 'second ball' contest itself (loose-ball/duel) is a missing primitive.

family: Restarts and set-piece behavior
justification: Where second balls drop is a region the ball enters (relation_destination_entry), but defining the set-piece second-ball zone needs structured_zones around the delivery target.

family: Restarts and set-piece behavior
justification: set_piece_structure exposes the recognized restart and observed attacking outfield width/depth/centroid at the event anchor. It does not claim runner stations, near/far-post roles, or routine design.

family: Restarts and set-piece behavior
justification: set_piece_structure provides restart context and at-frame arrangement, but blocking/picking requires blocker/marker roles and interaction typing; no such primitive and no role taxonomy.

family: Restarts and set-piece behavior
justification: A clearing pass/release away from the box after a set piece is an action event with progression away from goal; controlled_pass_episode + set_piece_structure capture the context, but labeling it a defensive clearance needs a clearance action type.

family: Restarts and set-piece behavior
justification: Whether the set-piece possession continues is an outcome window: action_chain (same-possession ordered) following the restart_taken anchor plus change_across_anchor tells retained-vs-lost.

family: Restarts and set-piece behavior
justification: set_piece_structure exposes the recognized restart and observed defending outfield width/depth/centroid at the event anchor. It does not claim zonal/man marking, assignments, or routine design.

family: Restarts and set-piece behavior
justification: set_piece_structure provides restart context, but zonal/man marking scheme inference requires assigning each defender to a marked attacker or zone — a role/reference assignment the system lacks.

family: Restarts and set-piece behavior
justification: The outcome of the set piece (shot / cleared / retained / turnover within window) is an outcome-window composition over the restart anchor.

family: Restarts and set-piece behavior
justification: Routine signature / pattern typing across repeated set pieces requires pattern recognition over run/delivery configurations; set_piece_structure provides only event context and at-frame arrangement.
closest_supported_substitute: set_piece_structure + action_chain (sequence only, no routine signature)

family: Restarts and set-piece behavior
justification: The start of a runner's movement is detectable from velocity onset and set_piece_structure supplies restart context, but scoping it as a set-piece run still needs off-ball-run typing and attacker role/timing constraints.

family: Restarts and set-piece behavior
justification: set_piece_structure provides restart context and arrangement, but coordinated runner patterns (near-post peel, blocker-runner) require off-ball-run pattern typing with role assignment.

family: Restarts and set-piece behavior
justification: set_piece_structure exposes the observed arrangement at the taken restart, but a pre-delivery setup episode still needs a pre-restart setup window and station/marking characterization.

family: Restarts and set-piece behavior
justification: A short restart followed by passes is an ordered same-possession action_chain anchored on the restart_taken event; pass legs and forward progression are controlled_pass_episodes.

family: Restarts and set-piece behavior
justification: set_piece_structure now exposes the throw-in restart and observed at-frame arrangement, but typing a throw-in routine/pattern still requires routine-pattern recognition.

family: Learned probability, value, and counterfactual models
justification: Action value is a learned value model; no value/learned models are implemented.

family: Learned probability, value, and counterfactual models
justification: Novelty scoring requires a learned distribution over behavior to measure deviation from; learned models not available.

family: Learned probability, value, and counterfactual models
justification: Counterfactuals are explicitly in the missing learned-models family; recording counterfactual assumptions presupposes a counterfactual engine that does not exist.

family: Learned probability, value, and counterfactual models
justification: Enumerating carry options that were NOT taken and valuing them is counterfactual modeling; not implemented.

family: Learned probability, value, and counterfactual models
justification: Alternative-movement counterfactuals require a learned/counterfactual rollout; not implemented.

family: Learned probability, value, and counterfactual models
justification: Unplayed pass options with valuation is counterfactual + value modeling; not implemented. (Note: actual OPEN passing options geometrically would be partial, but counterfactual valuation is the defining requirement here.)

family: Learned probability, value, and counterfactual models
justification: Rolling out a hypothetical future state is a counterfactual simulation/learned-dynamics model; not implemented.

family: Learned probability, value, and counterfactual models
justification: Scoring decision quality requires a learned value/decision model with counterfactual baselines; not implemented.

family: Learned probability, value, and counterfactual models
justification: An 'expected' probability of box entry is a learned probability model. (Observed box_entry_within IS supported; the expected/probabilistic form is not.)

family: Learned probability, value, and counterfactual models
justification: Expected/probabilistic final-third entry is a learned model; the OBSERVED final-third-entry-within window is supported, the expectation is not.

family: Learned probability, value, and counterfactual models
justification: Defensive xG-against is a learned probability model; not implemented.

family: Learned probability, value, and counterfactual models
justification: xG is the canonical learned probability model called out as not implemented.

family: Learned probability, value, and counterfactual models
justification: Pass completion probability is explicitly called out as a learned model not implemented.

family: Learned probability, value, and counterfactual models
justification: Probability of retaining possession is a learned model; the OBSERVED retained-possession window is supported, the expectation is not.

family: Learned probability, value, and counterfactual models
justification: EPV is a learned value model; not implemented.

family: Learned probability, value, and counterfactual models
justification: Expected progression is a learned value/probability model; observed forward progression is supported, the expectation is not.

family: Learned probability, value, and counterfactual models
justification: Regret = value of best counterfactual minus chosen action; requires counterfactual + value models. Not implemented.

family: Learned probability, value, and counterfactual models
justification: Expected shot creation is a learned probability model; observed shot-within is supported, the expectation is not.

family: Learned probability, value, and counterfactual models
justification: xT is a learned value model; not implemented.

family: Learned probability, value, and counterfactual models
justification: Calibration of a learned model presupposes a learned model exists to calibrate; none are implemented, so this is moot/missing.

family: Learned probability, value, and counterfactual models
justification: Domain-validity of a learned model presupposes a learned model; none implemented. (The general governance idea of validity is supported elsewhere, but this is scoped to learned models.)

family: Learned probability, value, and counterfactual models
justification: Valuing off-ball actions is a learned value model; not implemented.

family: Learned probability, value, and counterfactual models
justification: Predicting opponent response is a learned/counterfactual model; not implemented.

family: Learned probability, value, and counterfactual models
justification: A valued-space field (pitch control / value surface) is a learned/probabilistic model; not implemented.

family: Learned probability, value, and counterfactual models
justification: A learned style embedding is a learned representation model; not implemented.

family: Learned probability, value, and counterfactual models
justification: Likelihood of a trajectory under a learned distribution requires a learned generative/probabilistic model; not implemented.

family: Evidence, replay, tests, and capability governance
justification: Policy over what capabilities may be authored is a governance meta-concept; the proof-carrying capability discipline (PASS/FAIL/UNKNOWN, contracts) already governs authorability.

family: Evidence, replay, tests, and capability governance
justification: Reporting stability of decision boundaries is a parameter-sensitivity governance artifact; the system already produces sensitivity/parameter reports.

family: Evidence, replay, tests, and capability governance
justification: Capability contracts are the core of the proof-carrying discipline that already exists.

family: Evidence, replay, tests, and capability governance
justification: Invariant tests over capabilities are part of the existing capability-test / PASS-FAIL-UNKNOWN machinery.

family: Evidence, replay, tests, and capability governance
justification: A trace of capability evaluation is the existing evidence/predicate trace mechanism.

family: Evidence, replay, tests, and capability governance
justification: Recording that a capability is absent is the honest UNKNOWN/gap reporting the system already does (e.g. 'rank-N not observed').

family: Evidence, replay, tests, and capability governance
justification: Recording provider/evidence conflicts is part of existing provenance/evidence governance.

family: Evidence, replay, tests, and capability governance
justification: Conformance testing across providers is a capability-test artifact; the test/PASS-FAIL discipline exists. (Provider conformance is meta-system, not football.)

family: Evidence, replay, tests, and capability governance
justification: Explaining why a result degraded to UNKNOWN/partial is the existing unknown-reason / tracking_quality reporting.

family: Evidence, replay, tests, and capability governance
justification: Deprecation/migration of capabilities is a governance lifecycle concern handled by the capability-contract/versioning discipline that exists.

family: Evidence, replay, tests, and capability governance
justification: The set of entities supporting a result is an existing evidence field.

family: Evidence, replay, tests, and capability governance
justification: The frames supporting a result are existing evidence fields used for replay.

family: Evidence, replay, tests, and capability governance
justification: Geometry supporting a result is an existing evidence field.

family: Evidence, replay, tests, and capability governance
justification: Measurements supporting a result are existing evidence fields.

family: Evidence, replay, tests, and capability governance
justification: Gold-clip regression testing against frozen snapshots is part of the existing replay/test discipline.

family: Evidence, replay, tests, and capability governance
justification: Declaring which modalities a capability needs is part of the governance/contract discipline (and is how missing_modality is surfaced).

family: Evidence, replay, tests, and capability governance
justification: Parameter sensitivity reporting is an existing governance artifact.

family: Evidence, replay, tests, and capability governance
justification: Compute/performance budgets are a system-governance concern the runtime governs; meta-system, treated as supported.

family: Evidence, replay, tests, and capability governance
justification: Tracing predicate evaluation is the core proof-carrying trace mechanism that exists.

family: Evidence, replay, tests, and capability governance
justification: Provenance tracing is an existing data-provenance/evidence capability.

family: Evidence, replay, tests, and capability governance
justification: Bundling a replay with overlays is part of the existing replay capability over frozen snapshots/evidence frames.

family: Evidence, replay, tests, and capability governance
justification: A bounded replay window is part of the existing replay machinery.

family: Evidence, replay, tests, and capability governance
justification: Stable identity/hashing of a result enables replay and frozen-snapshot comparison; part of existing discipline.

family: Evidence, replay, tests, and capability governance
justification: The strength of claim (observed vs proxy vs candidate) is exactly the honest PASS/FAIL/UNKNOWN + proxy-labeling discipline the system already enforces.

family: Evidence, replay, tests, and capability governance
justification: Testing capabilities against synthetic scenarios is part of the existing capability-test discipline.

family: Evidence, replay, tests, and capability governance
justification: Recording why a result is UNKNOWN is the core honest-UNKNOWN reporting that exists.

family: Ball contact, control, contest, and possession
justification: Ball height (ground/air) is not modeled; tracking provides planar positions and velocity but no vertical/height state. No ground_air primitive in catalog.

family: Ball contact, control, contest, and possession
justification: Whether the ball is moving/stationary and its motion is derivable from ball velocity over tracking.

family: Ball contact, control, contest, and possession
justification: Ball leaving the field is a region-exit / boundary crossing detectable from ball position vs pitch bounds; an out event also anchors via action_event_anchor.

family: Ball contact, control, contest, and possession
justification: A ball-contact (touch) event is not a catalog primitive; controlled_pass_episode and one_touch_relay infer completed passes but a generic player-ball contact requires a touch/contact primitive.

family: Ball contact, control, contest, and possession
justification: A contest between players for the ball is a duel/contest with no primitive; tracking proximity alone cannot establish a contest.

family: Ball contact, control, contest, and possession
justification: Gaining control corresponds to a possession start, observable via change_across_anchor / possession_segment boundaries; but pinpointing the control-GAIN touch needs a touch/contact primitive.

family: Ball contact, control, contest, and possession
justification: Losing control is a possession end / turnover, observable via change_across_anchor; but a miscontrol-specific loss (vs a tackled/intercepted loss) needs a touch/contest primitive to disambiguate.

family: Ball contact, control, contest, and possession
justification: Whether the ball is dead (out of play / pre-restart) is given by the restart/event anchor and ball motion; action_event_anchor marks dead-ball periods.

family: Ball contact, control, contest, and possession
justification: A duel is a defensive/physical contest with no primitive (no duel/tackle/interception primitive per guidance).

family: Ball contact, control, contest, and possession
justification: A loose/uncontrolled ball state (no team in control) is not a catalog primitive; possession_segment assumes control. Requires a loose-ball primitive.

family: Ball contact, control, contest, and possession
